title: Majority Element
description:
Given an array `nums` of size `n`, return the majority element — the value
that appears more than `n / 2` times. You may assume it always exists.

solutions.summary: Maintain a candidate and a counter that cancels out minority votes.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature, returning the first element.

steps.title: Track a candidate and a count
steps.explanation: Start with no candidate and a zero counter.

code.python:
def majority_element(nums):
    count = 0
    candidate = nums[0]
    return candidate

steps.title: Pick a new candidate when count hits zero
steps.explanation: If the running count is zero, adopt the current value as the candidate.

steps.title: Count votes for and against
steps.explanation: Matching values add to the count; others subtract. The majority survives.

solutions.summary: Count occurrences until one exceeds n/2.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature, returning the first element.

steps.title: Create a counts map
steps.explanation: Track how many times each value has been seen.

steps.title: Tally and check each value
steps.explanation: Increment the count; once it passes `n/2`, that value is the majority.
